title: rockhausenia cornea
intro: Rockhausenia cornea, also known as rock alumwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as wounds, burns, and eczema, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of rockhausenia cornea include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce swelling, and soothe skin irritations. Rockhausenia cornea is often prepared as a topical ointment or salve, and can also be consumed as a tea or added to herbal remedies, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
